jokes.text: Andy claiming to be 'the first office Santa ever to make holiday wishes come true'
jokes.setup: Andy presents himself as a revolutionary Santa figure
jokes.punchline: The absurd grandiosity of claiming to be the 'first' office Santa to grant wishes
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy self-importance, well-integrated with his character's need to be special and revolutionary about mundane things

jokes.text: Stanley's list of weird Christmas themes: 'A Honolulu Christmas, a Pulp Fiction Christmas, a Muslim Christmas, Moroccan Christmas, Mo Rocca Christmas'
jokes.setup: Stanley complaining about weird thematic Christmases
jokes.punchline: The escalation from legitimate themes to 'Mo Rocca Christmas' - a random comedian
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with the Mo Rocca punchline being completely unexpected and absurd. Perfect Stanley grumpiness.

jokes.text: 'Christmas is Christmas is Christmas is Christmas'
jokes.setup: After listing absurd Christmas themes
jokes.punchline: Stanley's frustrated repetition expressing his desire for simple Christmas
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley delivery - the repetition shows his exasperation perfectly

jokes.text: 'I got Stanley tickets to see Lewis Black for his birthday. Might not have been the best idea'
jokes.setup: Andy giving Stanley Lewis Black tickets
jokes.punchline: The realization that giving an angry comedian to an already grumpy person wasn't wise
jokes.explanation: Nice character awareness joke - Andy realizing his gift choice may have backfired

jokes.text: 'I want Christmas. Just give me plain baby-Jesus-lying-in-a-manger Christmas'
jokes.setup: Stanley's frustration with themed Christmases
jokes.punchline: His specific, almost desperate plea for traditional Christmas
jokes.explanation: The specificity of 'baby-Jesus-lying-in-a-manger' makes this funnier than just 'traditional Christmas'

jokes.text: Andy giving Jim property on the moon 'Directly adjacent to the Sea of Tranquility. Beachfront.'
jokes.setup: Jim asking about moon property location
jokes.punchline: Andy calling lunar property 'beachfront' when there's no water
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor with the 'beachfront' moon property concept

jokes.text: 'She's not your grandmother is she?' 'Gam-Gam's name is Ruth, Jim'
jokes.setup: Jim asking if Jessica is Andy's grandmother
jokes.punchline: Andy's serious correction about his grandmother's actual name
jokes.explanation: Andy's earnest correction when Jim is obviously joking shows his social obliviousness

jokes.text: 'Only thing that's not serious by the way, our repartee'
jokes.setup: Andy describing his serious girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Using the pretentious word 'repartee' to describe their banter
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy pretentiousness - using 'repartee' when 'joking around' would suffice

jokes.text: 'I love Jessica, and I haven't even met her yet. It's like, we don't even need to meet, you know? I already love you. Stay home'
jokes.setup: Erin talking about loving Jessica without meeting her
jokes.punchline: The direct address telling Jessica to stay home
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin awkwardness - the transition from loving Jessica to telling her to stay home is beautifully uncomfortable

jokes.text: Kelly offering to be mean to Jessica: 'It's really no problem. I was already planning on being mean to her'
jokes.setup: Kelly offering to help Andy by being mean to Jessica
jokes.punchline: Her casual admission she was already planning to be mean
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly cattiness - the casual way she admits to premeditated meanness

jokes.text: 'I feel like a kitten being cradled by a gorilla'
jokes.setup: Toby describing Kevin's attempt at comfort
jokes.punchline: The specific and vivid imagery of kitten and gorilla
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual metaphor that perfectly captures both Toby's fragility and Kevin's well-meaning clumsiness

jokes.text: Robert's analysis of Black Eyed Peas: 'It's rock and roll for people who don't like rock and roll. It's rap for people who don't like rap. It's pop for people who don't like pop'
jokes.setup: Robert expressing distaste for Black Eyed Peas
jokes.punchline: The escalating repetitive structure revealing the band appeals to no one
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structure and insight - the repetitive format builds to the realization that the band satisfies nobody. Highly quotable and accurate cultural criticism.

jokes.text: 'Okay, the weak always bully the strong, contrary to what you see in the media'
jokes.setup: Dwight defending himself against pranking accusations
jokes.punchline: His backwards logic about bullying dynamics
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight twisted logic - presenting himself as the victim despite being the aggressor

jokes.text: Andy saying 'Moe-nay' instead of 'Money'
jokes.setup: Andy trying to speak in a language Jim and Dwight understand
jokes.punchline: His pretentious pronunciation of 'money'
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy pretentiousness but relatively predictable

jokes.text: 'My ex is meeting my sex. Which is always scary. You know? And not just because you think they might talk about your penis. That's just part of it'
jokes.setup: Andy nervous about Erin meeting Jessica
jokes.punchline: The awkward oversharing about penis discussion fears
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy oversharing creating discomfort - the penis comment is unexpected and inappropriate

jokes.text: Erin's scoliosis explanation and spontaneous demonstration
jokes.setup: Erin explaining her scoliosis and back brace
jokes.punchline: Her physical demonstration of 'spontaneous scoliosis'
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment with Erin's characteristic awkward oversharing

jokes.text: Darryl's popsicle explanation: 'Yeah, like, upstairs people 'Cause you got a stick up your butt'
jokes.setup: Someone asking about popsicles reference to upstairs people
jokes.punchline: Darryl's literal interpretation of 'stick up your butt'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting popsicle sticks to the idiom about uptight people

jokes.text: 'I will look so handsome for you, Darryl'
jokes.setup: Discussion of dressing up for the upstairs party
jokes.punchline: The unexpected romantic declaration to Darryl
jokes.explanation: Brief awkward moment suggesting romantic feelings toward Darryl

jokes.text: 'I get really pranky when I drink'
jokes.setup: Jim realizing he can't drink because of the bonus threat
jokes.punchline: The made-up word 'pranky' describing his drunk behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character insight with the invented word 'pranky' - very Jim-like

jokes.text: Meredith's elaborate shot peer pressure speech to Erin
jokes.setup: Offering Erin a shot
jokes.punchline: The elaborate philosophical justification for taking a shot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith - turning peer pressure into pseudo-philosophical adult empowerment

jokes.text: Jim giving fake credit card numbers over the phone as bait for Dwight
jokes.setup: Jim loudly sharing credit card information
jokes.punchline: The revelation it's bait for Dwight to commit credit card fraud
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for the payoff - classic Jim elaborate prank planning

jokes.text: 'So Dwight did take the bait. He used my credit card numbers to send a $200 bouquet of flowers to my wife. From me. Boom'
jokes.setup: The credit card bait from earlier
jokes.punchline: Dwight accidentally doing something nice for Jim by sending flowers to Pam
jokes.explanation: Perfect prank payoff - Dwight's attempt to hurt Jim backfires beautifully by doing something romantic. The 'Boom' delivery is perfect.

jokes.text: 'You usually dress like a Ghostbuster'
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on Robert's appearance at the party
jokes.punchline: The specific comparison to Ghostbuster attire
jokes.explanation: Sharp, specific insult with good visual imagery

jokes.text: Dwight's porcupine attack story with 'toothbrush and tooth powder'
jokes.setup: Dwight claiming to be attacked by a porcupine in his desk
jokes.punchline: The old-fashioned 'tooth powder' detail
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight character detail - of course he uses tooth powder instead of toothpaste

jokes.text: Toby's Chad Flenderman description: 'Just an easy-going black guy who knows the streets, yet he also went to Oxford. So just as comfortable on a motorcycle as he is on Air Force One. And he's also the world's leading Egyptologist'
jokes.setup: Toby describing his detective novel character
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous and contradictory character traits
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of absurd character traits that perfectly captures Toby's fantasy life and poor writing abilities. The progression from street knowledge to Oxford to Air Force One to Egyptology is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: 'Toby, nobody cares about your sex-crazed black detective' 'No, no, no, no, no women chase him. He misses his wife'
jokes.setup: Someone calling Chad Flenderman sex-crazed
jokes.punchline: Toby's earnest correction that he's actually loyal and misses his wife
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby earnestness defending his character's virtue - shows his fundamental decency even in fantasy

jokes.text: 'Porcupines don't have souls. They're like dogs'
jokes.setup: Discussion about calling animal control for the porcupine
jokes.punchline: Angela's casual declaration that porcupines are soulless like dogs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela line - the casual cruelty and the specific theology about animal souls

jokes.text: 'That's how the cool kids say "get a drink." - reverse spit'
jokes.setup: Jessica explaining her slang
jokes.punchline: The gross and nonsensical 'reverse spit' terminology
jokes.explanation: Shows Jessica's try-hard coolness with made-up slang that doesn't make sense

jokes.text: Erin's death wish for Jessica: 'I wish she was in a graveyard under the ground with worms coming out of her mouth'
jokes.setup: Erin making a Christmas wish about Jessica
jokes.punchline: The increasingly dark and specific death imagery
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation from seemingly sweet Erin to genuinely disturbing imagery. The specific details make it funnier and more unsettling. Classic cringe comedy at its best.

jokes.text: 'So which one are you, a murderer or a liar?'
jokes.setup: Andy trying to backtrack on his promise to fulfill wishes
jokes.punchline: Erin's logical trap forcing Andy to choose between murder and dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical trap - Erin's sweet demeanor delivering this dark ultimatum is perfectly uncomfortable

jokes.text: Jim's fake concern over the defaced photo of Cece, then revealing it was probably an accident by him
jokes.setup: Jim showing everyone a defaced photo of his daughter
jokes.punchline: His casual admission that he probably did it himself by accident
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - building up outrage before the deflating admission of personal responsibility

jokes.text: Andy's philosophical question about friends who aren't sleeping together staying friends
jokes.setup: Andy asking about friendship dynamics
jokes.punchline: The convoluted double-negative logic
jokes.explanation: Andy's confused logic is characteristic but the joke is somewhat muddled and hard to follow

jokes.text: 'Jessica, did you just fart?' 'And that is how it's done'
jokes.setup: Kelly asking Jessica about farting
jokes.punchline: Jessica's proud declaration of her fart as an accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Basic cringe humor - Jessica's confidence about farting fits her try-hard personality

jokes.text: Robert's description of Chad Flenderman: 'Chad Flenderman's kryptonite... We got a real Clarence Thomas here'
jokes.setup: Discussing Toby's detective character
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate Clarence Thomas comparison
jokes.explanation: Robert making an inappropriate political/sexual harassment reference fits his character but isn't especially clever

jokes.text: Meredith's treasures vs junk argument: 'It's my valuables' 'It's junk' 'No, they're my treasures' 'You're a hoarder'
jokes.setup: Meredith defending her collection of items
jokes.punchline: The escalation from valuables to treasures to hoarding accusation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Meredith's denial about her obvious hoarding tendencies

jokes.text: Dwight talking to the porcupine Henrietta about bonus money
jokes.setup: Dwight in the car with the porcupine
jokes.punchline: His casual conversation with the animal about sharing bonus money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - treating a porcupine as a business partner is exactly his kind of weird

jokes.text: 'Meredith, shut your drunk hole right now'
jokes.setup: Meredith being loud and drunk in an upscale neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Andy's specific insult 'drunk hole'
jokes.explanation: Good specific insult that captures Andy's frustration and Meredith's condition

jokes.text: Robert's predatory 'You thinking what I'm thinking?' to drunk Erin
jokes.setup: Robert alone with very drunk Erin
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate suggestion/question
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable moment that relies more on creepiness than clever writing. Shows character but isn't particularly well-crafted comedy.

jokes.text: 'Oh, man I was supposed to tell Dwight something. Come on, Jim'
jokes.setup: The entire episode about the bonus threat
jokes.punchline: Andy's casual realization he forgot to tell Dwight about the lifted threat
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Andy's forgetfulness undermining his own management strategy
